prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens on a very dark, low-contrast image in which the setting is not clearly visible. Only vague rectangular shapes can be made out, possibly an interior or a wall, but the visual content is indistinct.

It then cuts to a bold title-card style image. Large white capital letters fill the center of the frame, reading: “DON’T MAKE THE OCTANE MISTAKE.” The background appears blurred and streaked, suggesting motion or a roadway-like backdrop, but no specific objects are clearly identifiable behind the text.

Later, the commercial shows an outdoor driving scene in black and white. A light-colored car is seen from the rear, positioned on a road or driveway. It appears to be moving away or traveling along the road. Trees and shrubs line the right side, and the horizon or roadside landscape is visible on the left. The overall setting looks suburban or semi-rural.

The final sampled frame shows a Mobil service-station sign or display. The word “Mobil” appears prominently at the top in large letters. Below it is a display area containing a bright, detailed image that looks like an engine or engine-related mechanical components. The surrounding structure resembles part of a gas station or service-station forecourt.
